chaptername: Preface: Supplication for the Blessing of Living Buddha Lian-sheng
chapterbody: m, Ah, Hum. With reverence I offer my purified body, speech and mind to Mahavairocana. The Holy Buddha Locana is the Dharma body, Padmakumara is the bliss body. Living Buddha Lian-sheng is the emanation body. These three bodies being the same in essence as the Buddha's grace.
chapterbody 2: Homage to his True Buddha lineage and to his transcendent power which encompasses the whole universe. Radiating light throughout the three times, he can manifest himself instantaneously. Disciples of the Buddha should constantly cry out for his pure light, which magnifies virtue and wisdom.
chapterbody 3: In the past, his Buddhahood was prophesied by Shakyamuni Buddha. Amitabha Buddha entrusted him with the mission of salvation. Maitreya Bodhisattva bestowed on him the red crown. Guru Padmasambhava taught him the tantra. We pray you never abandon your past vows to liberate us all. As you enfold us in protection and care, empower us to quickly attain realization.
chapterbody 5: Namo Mahavairocana Buddha.
chapterbody 6: Namo Buddha Locana.
chapterbody 7: Namo Padmakumara.
chapterbody 8: Namo Living Buddha Lian-sheng.
chapterbody 9: Namo the True Buddha Assembly, all buddhas, bodhisattvas, and mahasattvas everywhere throughout the three times.
chapterbody 13: Do not underestimate this short prayer of supplication. If one is able to recite this passage prior to cultivation, the merit will be as deep as the ocean and the Dharma power will be as towering as Mt. Sumeru. One who recites this supplication every day will manifest inconceivable power.

chapterbody 19: At noon on May 18 of the Yiyou Year, I, Living Buddha Lian-sheng Sheng-Yen Lu, was born into the human realm. At the age of 26, Jade Pond Golden Mother opened my divine sight.
chapterbody 20: I saw:
chapterbody 21: Jade Pond Golden Mother.
chapterbody 22: Amitabha Buddha.
chapterbody 23: Ksitigarbha Bodhisattva.
chapterbody 24: They bestowed on me these words: 'Single-mindedly study Buddhism. Single-mindedly practice. Single-mindedly perform good deeds.' Lord Indra bestowed on me the two words 'Loyalty and Righteousness.' I was later guided to personally see my predecessor, Padmakumara.
chapterbody 25: I then paid homage to eminent and highly virtuous monks, learned the Buddhadharma, and attained great accomplishment in Sutrayana and Vajrayana. I founded True Buddha School and raised the banner of Buddhadharma.
chapterbody 27: After attaining Buddhahood, I manifested supernatural power, was unhindered, possessed skillful means, brought the dead back to life, commanded wind and rain, cured illnesses and banished demons, emanated countless bodies, ascended to heaven and entered into the earth, and became the guru of celestial and human beings.
chapterbody 28: Wherever the banner of Buddhadharma is, the Dharma rain falls. More than five million sentient beings have been saved. More than 70 Lei Tsang Temples have been established. My footprints have been all over the world and five continents.
chapterbody 29: I bestowed empowerments on sentient beings, discoursed the Buddhadharma, and have delivered countless sentient beings. My activities have been utterly unprecedented.
chapterbody 30: (As for the above supplication, if one recites it daily, spiritual responses will come one after another. For this reason, it is an important key cultivation formula.)

These were all secret lineages received while meditating.
chapterbody 20: My ordination lineage is: In the past, when I was still a lay practitioner at Ling Shen Ching Tze Temple (LSCTT) in Seattle, USA, Reverend Guoxian from Wai Chuen Monastery in Hong Kong came to take refuge in me. Reverend Guoxian stayed in Seattle for a long time. He encouraged me to receive ordination. I was therefore ordained at the True Buddha Tantric Quarter on the 10th day of the second month of the lunar calendar at the age of 43. For this reason, Reverend Guoxian is both my disciple and my ordination master, a dual-role legend as both disciple and ordination master.

chapterbody 9: As the Buddha was about to enter parinirvana, Manjusri Bodhisattva descended and said, 'Please turn the Dharma wheel!'
chapterbody 10: The Buddha replied, 'Have I ever turned the Dharma wheel?'
chapterbody 11: Upon hearing this, Manjusri attained enlightenment.
chapterbody 12: (The Buddha's reply 'Have I ever turned the Dharma wheel?' is the ultimate attainment of Zen. I understand the meaning. Do you, my holy disciples, also understand?)
chapterbody 13: Also:
chapterbody 14: A bodhisattva asked Manjusri, 'How many sentient beings have you saved?'
chapterbody 15: Manjusri replied, 'Three steps forward. Three steps backward.'
chapterbody 16: (Manjusri's 'Three steps forward. Three steps backward.' is also the ultimate enlightenment of Zen. I understand the meaning. Do you, my holy disciples, also understand?)
chapterbody 20: Someone asked me, 'Grandmaster! Just what are you enlightened to?'
chapterbody 21: I replied, 'Once I say something, it will be wrong.'
chapterbody 22: They asked again, 'Please do express it in words.'
chapterbody 24: I answered:
chapterbody 25: Enlightened to nothing to gain, instantly severs afflictions.
chapterbody 26: Enlightened to no self, instantly severs self-grasping.
chapterbody 27: Enlightened to no birth, instantly ends death.
chapterbody 28: Enlightened to non-abiding, instantly ends attachment.
chapterbody 29: Enlightened to no human beings, instantly severs conflict.
chapterbody 30: Enlightened to no sentient beings, instantly severs desire for fame and reputation.
chapterbody 31: Enlightened to no longevity, instantly severs birth and death.

chaptername: 004 The Key Cultivation Formula of the Path of Accumulation
chapterbody: n cultivation, I believe one should practice the Path of Accumulation first. What is the Path of Accumulation? To put it in a nutshell, it's the accumulation one requires to cultivate from the mundane state to Buddhahood.

chapterbody 4: In addition to continuing the good works of Sheng-Yen Lu Foundation, Shimu Master Lianxiang and I have developed another long-term plan. We have founded an Emergency Relief Fund. After we pass away, the provisions Shimu and I leave behind will be in the bank. The monthly interest will be used for a particular kind of charity, that is, when a patient cannot afford medical expenses for treatment at a hospital, the Emergency Relief Fund will help pay for it.
chapterbody 5: I perceive sickness to be the most extreme suffering of sentient beings. To be able to provide financial assistance to someone who is sick and needs to see a doctor but is unable to afford the overwhelming medical expenses carries significance.
chapterbody 6: We have already initiated this plan, but for the long term, there must be corresponding measures as well. This is 'helping others in an emergency because of one's heart and mind!' This act we have performed is namely the Path of Accumulation.

chapterbody 13: Do not perform evil deeds. Evil deeds are like black clouds.
chapterbody 14: White clouds and black clouds both obscure the sun.
chapterbody 15: What they mean is do not create white karma (good karma) and do not create black karma (evil karma).
chapterbody 16: Do not create either good karma or evil karma so the Buddha-nature will emerge.
chapterbody 18: In my opinion:
chapterbody 19: Performing good deeds is the Path of Accumulation. Only by accumulating merit can one create the cause to learn Buddhadharma. Only by accumulating merit can one encounter a virtuous guru to teach one the true and authentic Buddhadharma. I have observed the bright light radiating from a good and virtuous person. Where there is virtuous light there is the cause for ascending to heaven. When that person's life is over, they will enjoy happiness in heaven. If this person is in the human realm, they will be prosperous with good fortune, and with countless benefactors to help them. A good and virtuous person will not fall into the three evil realms.
chapterbody 20: Only one who has the compassion of the buddhas of the three times is qualified to be a protector of sentient beings in the ten directions. A benevolent guru whose great kindness to sentient beings is like a mountain is actually a buddha or bodhisattva returning to the human realm with great compassion. This bodhisattva's mercy is a great virtue.

chaptername: 005 The True Practice of Fourfold Refuge
chapterbody: he foremost practice of the Four-Preliminaries Practice is the Fourfold Refuge Practice. Why do I add the word 'true'? Because the Fourfold Refuge Practice produces power.
chapterbody 2: 1. Enter the shrine.
chapterbody 3: 2. Sit upright.
chapterbody 4: 3. Visualize the face of the Root Guru. A white syllable 'om' is located on his brow point, a red syllable 'ah' is located on his throat, and a blue syllable 'hum' is located on his heart.
chapterbody 5: 4. Visualize the three lights shining onto practitioner's brow point, throat, and heart respectively.
chapterbody 6: 5. Recite 'Namo Living Buddha Lian-sheng Grandmaster Lu Root Guru' (three times).
chapterbody 7: 6. Recite 'Namo Buddha, Dharma, and Sangha, the Three Eternal Jewels of the Ten Directions'. (three times)
chapterbody 8: 7. Recite the Fourfold Refuge Mantra:

chapterbody 10: Key cultivation formula: While reciting the Fourfold Refuge Mantra, visualize the Guru, Buddha, Dharma, and Sangha emitting five-colored light shining on the practitioner. At the same time, nectar from the Guru, Buddha, Dharma, and Sangha flows into and empowers the practitioner. All the practitioner's black karmic hindrances and dark qi exit from the practitioner's skin pores. Sickness transforms into yellow-colored water and flows out from the practitioner. Centipedes, scorpions, and tiny black insects transformed from black magic spells crawl out from the practitioner's body. The practitioner totally relaxes. The mind and spirit become pure and peaceful. The practitioner's good fortune and wisdom increase.

chapterbody 24: I want to clarify that some think Four-Preliminaries Practice is a minor practice of Vajrayana. Actually, they are entirely mistaken. I will set out the practice for everyone to see that it's truly incredible!
chapterbody 25: In fact, the Fourfold Refuge Practice is very powerful. It consists of mudra, visualization, mantra, the Root Guru, and the Three Jewels of the ten directions and three times. One can cultivate this practice by itself. During group cultivation in a temple, cultivation venue, or cultivation association, this practice is simplified to the point it can't be further simplified. Only the Fourfold Refuge Mantra is recited, and the rest is completely omitted.
chapterbody 26: I am telling you, when the 'True Fourfold Refuge Practice' is cultivated all the way to fruition, the Root Guru will appear before one and one will attain spiritual union with the guru. When one attains spiritual union with the guru, one attains spiritual union with the Buddha, Dharma, and Sangha of the ten directions and three times. One is able to ascend to the pure land.
chapterbody 28: I frequently say:
chapterbody 29: The Four-Preliminaries Practice is a major dharma. The Fourfold Refuge Practice is therefore a major dharma. If during one's lifetime, one only cultivates the Fourfold Refuge Practice, one will never lose one's bodhicitta.
chapterbody 30: Those who single-mindedly enter this samadhi will be equally able to reach attainment. This is absolutely not a falsehood.

chaptername: 006 True Mandala Offering Practice
chapterbody: say: As a result of cultivating the True Mandala Offering Practice, I (Grandmaster Lu) have more than everything I need in this life and this will also be true in future lives.
chapterbody 2: I can say that in the future, if it is my fate to be reborn in the human realm, I will be the wealthiest person in the human realm. I say this with confidence: I will absolutely be the richest person in the human realm. There will be none who can compare with me.
chapterbody 4: I am describing the Mandala Offering Practice as follows:
chapterbody 5: 1. Form the mudra.
chapterbody 6: The mudra is the Offering Mudra. The guru will instruct how to form the Offering Mudra. (Lin Yun used to call this mudra the Invoking Good Fortune Mudra)
chapterbody 7: Mudra formation: Interlace fingers (with hands back to back) so that palms and fingers face upwards. Hook the index finger of the left hand over the middle finger of the right. Hook the index finger of the right hand over the middle finger of the left. Press the thumb of the left hand over the little finger of the right. Press the thumb of the right hand over the little finger of the left. Manipulate the ring fingers so that they stand vertically, back to back.
chapterbody 8: Explanation of the mudra: The Offering Mudra represents Mt. Sumeru and the Four Continents. (Purvavideha in the East, Jambudvipa in the South, Aparagodaniya in the West, Uttarakuru in the North).
chapterbody 9: It represents the entire universe.
chapterbody 11: 2. Visualize the offerings in the mudra.
chapterbody 12: Some practitioners place a few grains of rice in the left palm. In my personal practice, I visualize the offerings in my palms.
chapterbody 13: Offerings: Food. Flowers, incense, lamps, tea, fruit, water, and soapy water. Wish-fulfilling conch. Seven Treasures. Eight Jewels. (Diamonds, gold, silver) Dharma implements, clothing.
chapterbody 15: 3. Offering visualization:
chapterbody 16: Visualize the mudra transforming into the entire universe and all of space. (Key cultivation formula) Visualize the offerings transforming to be as massive as clouds and the sea. (Key cultivation formula) Visualize the offerings filling the entire universe. (Key cultivation formula)
chapterbody 18: 4. Recite the mantra:
chapterbody 19: Om, sa-er-wa, da-ta-ga-da, ee-da-mooh, guru lana, man-cha'la, kan, nee-lee-yeh, dah-yah-mee
chapterbody 20: Upon finishing the mantra recitation, touch one's crown with the mudra, and release the mudra.
chapterbody 22: 5. Fifth, recite the Praise
chapterbody 23: Mount Meru together with the Four Continents, sun and moon,
chapterbody 24: Transforms into precious treasures to offer to the buddhas.

chapterbody 26: Quickly remove negative karma to realize Buddhahood.
chapterbody 30: I would like to tell you that: The offerings of this mundane world can include food as well as one's clothing, housing, and transportation. However, these are mundane things that are low, crude, and vulgar. What are high class offerings? Cultivation offerings (Dharma offerings). Body, speech, and mind (purity offerings). Qi, channels, and light drops (the highest class offerings). Great bliss, light, emptiness (offering of sensations). This is the most important key cultivation formula.

chaptername: 007 Great Bliss Offering (Secret)
chapterbody: his offering belongs to the 'offering of sensations'. Few people know about it and are able to perform it. Therefore, only 'adepts' are able to make this kind of offering, the offering of 'Highest Yoga Tantra'. It is therefore referred to as 'secret'.
chapterbody 2: One who is qualified to be called an 'adept' should have the following capabilities, enlightened to the absolute truth, proven non-leakage, and ability to move qi through the central channel, ascend inner fire and descend light drops. As a result, when an adept practitioner is in union with his consort, the friction caused by the movement of qi, inner fire, and light drops produces indescribable great bliss.
chapterbody 4: I will tell you:
chapterbody 5: Ordinary sexual activity between men and women results in 'small bliss'. Only the union of an adept with his consort is able to produce great bliss, which lasts the entire day, or for a prolonged period, and with tingling ecstasy throughout one's entire body. (Indescribable).
chapterbody 6: At this time, the practitioner makes this 'great bliss offering' to the goddesses guarding the four directions, the dakinis of the eight directions, the 20 celestial maidens, and all other deities. In one's mind, transform 'great bliss' to 'dual body' and spread throughout all of space. All the dakinis are filled with joy.
chapterbody 10: As for the consort, because of the ability of the practitioner, the channels of the practitioner and the consort are able to connect with each other, their qi is able to flow through, and their light drops merge. Consequently, the consort also produces indescribable bliss that permeates her entire body, prolonged and abundant.
chapterbody 11: The consort then offers this 'great bliss' to buddhas, bodhisattvas, vajra deities, Dharma protectors, dakinis, and the heavens. (All of these are also filled with joy.)
chapterbody 15: I, Grandmaster Lu, can tell everyone for a fact that this bliss is not mortal small bliss. It is the great bliss arising from cultivation. (Male practitioners must fully master non-leakage). Because light drops merge with the inner fire, the channel and chakras are opened and emit light. This light is rare and truly incredible. One's Buddha-nature manifests from this light.
chapterbody 16: I have personally witnessed the descent of Prajnaparamita, Vajravarahi, Simhamukha, Sarasvati, and Nairatmya. Countless unimaginable and incredible goddesses also descended.

chaptername: 009 True Vajra-heart Bodhisattva Practice
chapterbody: his dharma is the most important practice in Vajrayana. All Vajrayana practitioners are required to cultivate this practice.

chapterbody 16: The Four-Preliminaries Practice is a major practice.
chapterbody 17: When this practice is cultivated to the full extent, upon reaching spiritual union, one will never lose one's bodhicitta.
chapterbody 18: Those who lost faith in the Buddhadharma had never attained spiritual union with the Four-Preliminaries Practice, not even basic spiritual union.
chapterbody 19: Those who have attained spiritual union with the Four-Preliminaries Practice must have reached fruition.
chapterbody 21: As long as one has attained spiritual union with the Four Preliminaries, one will easily attain spiritual union with Personal Deity Practice, inner practice (qi, channels, and light drops), Dharma Protector Practice, Highest Yoga Tantra, and the Great Perfection. In other words, attainment of spiritual union with one dharma is attainment of spiritual union with all dharmas, and one will accomplish the fruit of enlightenment.

chapterbody 32: All my transgressions committed since time immemorial

(3 times) Visualize Vajra-heart Bodhisattva sitting on an eight-petaled lotus. He is white and wears a Five-buddha Crown, celestial garment, and skirts. His right hand holds a vajra scepter against his heart while his left hand holds a vajra bell by his left thigh.
chapterbody 41: In his heart is a syllable 'hum'. White bright nectar within his heart flows out to empower the practitioner and transforms practitioner's negative karma into black water which is expelled through the skin pores. Upon the practitioner being purified, Vajra-heart Bodhisattva enters the practitioner's body (merging of self and deity). Vajra-heart Bodhisattva and practitioner become one without distinction between the two.

The greatest sign of repentance is that Vajra-heart Bodhisattva manifests and says to the practitioner, 'My good and virtuous child, your purity is restored! Your three karmas are purified! You have attained spiritual union! And, you are a child of the buddhas! In the future, you will certainly attain Buddhahood!'

chaptername: 010 Armor Protection Practice (Key Cultivation Formula)
chapterbody: (Grandmaster Lu) received this practice from Master Pufang, in whom I had taken refuge. His Dharma name is Yuanchi.
chapterbody 3: The principal deity of Armor Protection Practice is Vajrapani Bodhisattva. Tibetans address this deity as Hand Vajra. Vajrapani Bodhisattva, Avalokitesvara Bodhisattva, and Manjusri Bodhisattva are referred to as the Three Protectors. Avalokitesvara Bodhisattva represents compassion, Manjusri Bodhisattva represents wisdom, and Vajrapani Bodhisattva represents Dharma power.
chapterbody 7: Vajrapani Bodhisattva Mudra:
chapterbody 8: Place the palms together. Bend the ring fingers inward and interlace them. Point the index fingers apart.
chapterbody 9: One may also form the Vajra Mudra, which is to place the palms together and interlace the fingers.
chapterbody 13: Recite the mantra:
chapterbody 14: 'Om, bo ru lan ze lee.'
chapterbody 15: While reciting the mantra, touch the mudra to the forehead, throat, heart, left shoulder, and right shoulder.
chapterbody 16: Visualize oneself transforming into Vajrapani Bodhisattva in wrathful form. His right hand holds a vajra sceptre up high while his left hand holds a vajra lasso. His legs lunge forward. (This is the Armor Protection Body Mudra)
chapterbody 20: Grandmaster Lu believes that Armor Protection Practice can be widely used in daily life.
chapterbody 21: Food—Form the Vajrapani Mudra and virtually touch one's food with the mudra. Recite 'Om, bo ru lan ze lee'. One's food is then purified.
chapterbody 22: Clothing—Form the Vajrapani Mudra and virtually touch the clothes one is wearing with the mudra. Recite 'Om, bo ru lan ze lee'. One's clothes then become 'armor' which can protect one from demons.
chapterbody 23: Residences—Form the Vajrapani Mudra and virtually touch the four walls of one's house with the mudra. Recite 'Om, bo ru lan ze lee'. This establishes a protective boundary around one's residence or bedroom. No ghosts or evil spirits can enter. One's residence will be peaceful.
chapterbody 24: Transportation—Form the Vajrapani Mudra and virtually touch a car, boat, plane, motorcycle, or bicycle with the mudra. Recite 'Om, bo ru lan ze lee'. One will be safe when out on the road, with no accidents or disasters.

chapterbody 28: When we learn Buddhadharma, we must learn both theory and actual practice. One must know how to apply Buddhist principles.

chaptername: 012 The True Personal Deity Practice
chapterbody: or this personal deity practice, we will use Amitabha Buddha as an example.
chapterbody 2: Preliminary Practice:

chapterbody 7: 4. Visualization:
chapterbody 8: Visualize, in the void, a 'bang' syllable transforming into a lotus. Within the lotus, an 'ah' syllable transforms into a moon disc. Within the moon disc, a 'seh' syllable transforms into Amitabha Buddha. Amitabha has one head and two arms, forms the Meditation Mudra, and sits on a lotus platform in the full-lotus position. White 'om' light shines onto one's brow point. Red 'ah' light shines onto one's throat. Blue 'hum' light shines onto one's heart. (Three-lights blessing)
chapterbody 9: Visualize 'merging of self and deity': Amitabha emits great light. Nectar flows out from this light empowering one. One is purified and liberated. Amitabha shrinks into a light point as small as a grain of rice, enters one's crown, and descends to the lotus within one's heart. Amitabha gradually enlarges, becoming the same size as one. One instantaneously transforms into Amitabha. (One and the same) One (Amitabha Buddha) then emits light throughout the ten directions, three times, and ten dharma realms, universally saving sentient beings.
chapterbody 10: 5. Mantra Recitation:

Light of the Dharma realm. Merging into one.

chaptername: 013 Key Cultivation Formula of Personal Deity Practice, Part 1
chapterbody: (Grandmaster Lu) believe: Personal deity practice is a crucial cultivation.
chapterbody 3: Many people have asked, 'Who is my personal deity?'
chapterbody 4: I always reply, 'The deity toward whom you feel affinity.'
chapterbody 6: 'What if I like a lot of different personal deities?'
chapterbody 7: 'Pick the one you like the most.'
chapterbody 9: 'Is it permissible to cultivate many different personal deities?' '
chapterbody 10: You may if you have time.'
chapterbody 12: I tell you for a fact. One is many. Many are one. Spiritual union in one practice is spiritual union with all the deities. This is the significance of the word 'oneness.'
chapterbody 16: Question: 'How does one invoke the deity to descend?'
chapterbody 17: Answer: 'Form the Invocation Mudra and recite the Invocation Verse.'
chapterbody 19: 'How does one form the Invocation Mudra?'
chapterbody 20: 'Vajra Hook Mudra. Form both hands into a first. Place the left fist under the right fist. Extend the left forefinger, and clasp it with the right hand. Extend the right forefinger and bend it like a hook.'
chapterbody 22: 'What is the Invocation Mantra?'
chapterbody 23: 'Om, bie-zha, sha-ma-ye, zha' or 'Om-ah-hum, so-ha'. Either mantra is acceptable.
chapterbody 25: 'What if the deity does not descend after invocation?'
chapterbody 27: Strengthen the power of mantra recitation by making it concise and forceful. Use the power of mind to visualize that a beam of white light emitted from Amitabha Buddha's brow point is revolving from right to left.'
chapterbody 28: (Important key cultivation formula)
chapterbody 32: Question: 'If one is unable to sever wandering thoughts, how does one enter samadhi?'
chapterbody 33: Answer: 'Not severing wandering thoughts is the mundane state, so one will not, of course, be able to enter samadhi. The key point is that mundane beings possess differentiation, love and hate, gain and loss. Do not allow the sense faculties of eye, ear, nose, tongue, body, and mind to arise. In this way, self-nature will manifest.'
chapterbody 35: 'Do you mean concentration?'
chapterbody 39: 'Transform the six thieves into a single mind.'
chapterbody 42: 'The dual use of cessation and visualization.'
chapterbody 45: Use the power of mind to invoke the deity.
chapterbody 53: Use the power of mudra to invoke the deity.
chapterbody 54: Use the power of mantra to invoke the deity. If the deity still does not descend, turn the Vajra Hook three times.

chaptername: 014 Key Cultivation Formula of Personal Deity Practice, Part 2
chapterbody: ajrayana practitioners must establish a shrine. The shrine should have Buddhas, bodhisattvas, Dharma protectors, sutras, stupa(s), an image of the guru, and so forth. The shrine should be pure and dignified. A practitioner should feel happy to spend time meditating there.
chapterbody 5: When cultivating, a Vajrayana practitioner should first perform a great prostration followed by sitting in the Seven-featured Vairocana Posture:
chapterbody 9: Choice of shrine:
chapterbody 10: A quiet room that is able to accumulate qi.
chapterbody 11: The direction of the shrine should be able to receive the water deity.
chapterbody 12: The magnetic field of the shrine should be in harmony with that of the practitioner.
chapterbody 13: The magnetic direction of the shrine should be in harmony with that of the practitioner.
chapterbody 17: For a Vajrayana practitioner, the key cultivation formula for attaining spiritual union with the personal deity is that the personal deity's image is identical to practitioner's image.
chapterbody 18: The color of practitioner's clothing should be in accord with the color of the personal deity.
chapterbody 19: Practitioner should hold the same Dharma implements as the personal deity.
chapterbody 20: Practitioner should have the personal deity's seed syllable in the heart.
chapterbody 21: Practitioner should recite the personal deity mantra 100,000 times.
chapterbody 22: Practitioner should keep the personal deity's image in mind at all times.
chapterbody 23: Practitioner's bodhi deeds should be the same as the personal deity's bodhi deeds.
chapterbody 24: Practitioner should have the same vows as the personal deity.
chapterbody 25: Practitioner's body, speech, and mind should be in harmony with that of the personal deity.
chapterbody 26: In summary, everything about the practitioner should be in accordance with the personal deity, such as food, clothing, housing, transportation, outwardness, inwardness, secretness, utmost secretness, everything perfectly consistent.
chapterbody 30: The Vajrayana practitioner must practice 'merging of self and deity'. The key cultivation formula is as follows:
chapterbody 31: Visualize, in the space in front of one, the personal deity moving above practitioner's crown. (Recite the personal deity's mantra 108 times)
chapterbody 32: Visualize the nectar of the personal deity filling practitioner's entire body. Practitioner attains purification. (Recite the personal deity's mantra 108 times)
chapterbody 33: Visualize the personal deity transforming into a point of light, descending from the crown to the heart, and coming to rest on the lotus in practitioner's heart. (Recite the personal deity's mantra 108 times)
chapterbody 34: Visualize the personal deity becoming gradually larger until the same size as practitioner and becoming one and the same as practitioner. (Recite the personal deity's mantra 108 times)
chapterbody 35: Finally, visualize practitioner becoming as vast as space, and that the entirety of space is practitioner. (Recite the personal deity's mantra 108 times)
chapterbody 39: When all this has been performed, spiritual union will be easy to attain. These are all important key cultivation formulas.
chapterbody 43: Place tongue at the top of the palate.
chapterbody 45: The seat should be slightly higher in the back.
chapterbody 49: Eyes should focus on the tip of the nose or straight ahead.
chapterbody 51: Breathing should be calm and regular.
chapterbody 53: Sit in the full-lotus position (most ideal). If one is unable to sit in this position, one may sit in the half-lotus position. Otherwise, one may sit in the Burmese position.

chaptername: 015 Key Cultivation Formula of Personal Deity Practice, Part 3
chapterbody: iving Buddha Lian-sheng Sheng-Yen Lu's vital key cultivation formula for entering samadhi:
chapterbody 3: According to the Ajatashatrukaukrityavinodana Sutra [The Buddha's Discourse on King Ajatashatru], King Ajatashatru desired to eradicate his karmic transgressions, so he prepared a bolt of priceless fabric as an offering to Manjusri Bodhisattva. Manjusri, however, was nowhere to be found.
chapterbody 4: From the void, the king heard Manjusri's voice saying, 'Make your offering to whomever you may see.' The king saw Auspicious Wisdom Bodhisattva and Arhat Mahakasyapa. He offered the bolt of fabric to them, but they declined saying, 'Since we have done nothing to deserve this offering, we cannot accept it.' They then immediately disappeared.
chapterbody 5: The king took the bolt of fabric and tried to offer it to 500 monks. The 500 monks simply vanished without a trace. The king was astounded. He subsequently offered the bolt of fabric to the queen by his side. To his surprise, the queen disappeared as well.
chapterbody 6: Now, the only person remaining was the king himself, holding the precious fabric and seeing nothing around him. King Ajatashatru then heard a voice saying 'Please offer the fabric to anybody you see.' Therefore, the king draped the bolt of fabric on his own back. To his surprise, the king himself also disappeared.
chapterbody 7: From space, Manjusri Bodhisattva said, 'If one lets go of all forms, all forms will disappear without a trace...' All of a sudden, King Ajatashatru attained this enlightenment.
chapterbody 11: This passage of the sutra is a great key cultivation formula. King Ajatashatru was the offeror. The bolt of fabric was the object offered. Manjusri was the one to whom the offering was made. However, the final result was no receiver, no offeror, and nothing offered (emptiness of the three wheels). Ultimately, there is nothing to gain.
chapterbody 12: Manjusri said, 'Bhagavan, the Buddha, had realized that all phenomena are non-arising like void, and cannot be contaminated.' This is the key cultivation formula for entering samadhi: no one meditating, no meditation, and no object of meditation. Because of this, one enters samadhi. (Entering samadhi with this dharma of 'emptiness of the three wheels' is the supreme dharma gate for attaining bodhi and nirvana.)
chapterbody 16: In this mundane world, I see that people are illusory, substances are illusory, I am illusory, and non-arising is like emptiness. Therefore, I have realized that millions upon billions of infinite buddhas of the ten directions attained nirvana and supreme bodhi all because of this. This is therefore inconceivable, incomparable, and indescribable. (There is originally no self. Phenomena arise according to circumstances.)

Thereafter, the practitioner will have the sensation of 'merging with the personal deity, transforming into the personal deity, and always experiencing the personal deity in close proximity' at all times (time) and in all places (space). One is never apart from the personal deity until attaining bodhi.

chaptername: 017 Key Cultivation Formula of Personal Deity Practice, Part 5
chapterbody: fter attaining spiritual union, I (Grandmaster Lu) experienced the following spiritual sensations:
chapterbody 3: 1. Upon entering meditation, I had the sensation of being as immovable as a mountain, towering as high as Mt. Sumeru. It was impossible to see the peak or the vast and infinite depths below. I myself was the universe of pure lands. My body was the universe. This spiritual sensation was like having all pure lands present in my mind.
chapterbody 4: 2. Upon entering meditation, the dharma flow of the personal deity flowed into my body, and my entire body was filled with ambrosia. My body, speech, and mind were purified. All the black karma accumulated through my previous lifetimes was transformed into black qi which exited through my pores and disappeared without a trace.
chapterbody 5: 3. Upon entering meditation, inner fire rose by itself, warm, red, and bright. From the soles of my feet to the crown of my head, and from the crown of my head to the soles of my feet, I felt the sensation of bliss. This bliss was not ordinary bliss. It was great bliss, brilliant light, and no thoughts. I sat within brilliant light. My entire body was filled with bliss. My afflictions vanished, I was unhindered, and I merged with dharma realms.
chapterbody 6: 4. Upon entering meditation, I could fly upward and extremely fast. I was able to travel to infinite pure lands. I could, of course, visit the pure land of the personal deity, the three realms of heavens, and Maha Twin Lotus Ponds. There was virtually nothing I couldn't do. I could be enormous or tiny, a man or a woman, soft or hard, a god or a ghost. I could be whatever I wanted to be, such as a fish in the water, an eagle in the sky, or a tiger in the forest, changing and transforming in any way whatsoever.
chapterbody 7: 5. Upon entering meditation, I was able to see the personal deity. I could hear the personal deity's Dharma discourse. I knew my past, present, and future. I could even know the past, present, and future of others and the thoughts of others. I had the power of mighty deities derived from buddhas, bodhisattvas, shravakas, pratyekabuddhas, or celestial deities. I was able to give people happiness and eradicate their suffering. The personal deity was always to my left and right. Or, I was the personal deity, absolute oneness.
chapterbody 8: 6. Upon entering meditation, any Buddhist sutra which I didn't understand was now understood. The absolute truth that I didn't understand was now understood. I attained omniscient knowledge of the mundane and supermundane worlds.
chapterbody 10: Phenomena which manifest in the mundane world are impermanent and constantly changing. Reincarnation is meaningless. Instead of indulging in profit or benefit in the world, one should cultivate the Buddha's teachings on liberation.

chaptername: 018 True Non-leakage Practice, Part 1
chapterbody: (Grandmaster Lu) can honestly tell everyone, 'I accomplished Non-leakage Practice long ago!' I have not experienced leakage of light drops (bodily fluids), day or night, for one year, 10 years, 20 years, 30 years... I am 'The Venerable of Non-leakage'.
chapterbody 2: I will give an example:
chapterbody 3: My sacral chakra has transformed into Taiwan's Shihmen Reservoir, which is tightly locked. Because no water has been discharged, the sluice gate has become too rusty to open. As a result, not a single drop has been lost.
chapterbody 4: This is, as said in Taoism, practicing to transform essence to qi, qi to spirit, spirit returning to emptiness, and emptiness returning to Tao. That I have long reached accomplishment in Non-leakage Practice is absolutely true and an actual fact.
chapterbody 8: I will add:
chapterbody 9: While the 10th Panchen Lama was in Beijing, he married a local girl. Later, a daughter was born. (This daughter studied in California). When I heard about it, I was speechless!
chapterbody 11: Moreover:
chapterbody 12: Thaye Dorje, one of the two 17th Karmapas, married his childhood sweetheart. Later, they also had a daughter. Once again, I was speechless when I learned about it.
chapterbody 16: Honestly, if I were an emperor of ancient times, as an accomplished practitioner of non-leakage, I would not be concerned about having 3,000 exquisitely beautiful concubines in the palace harem. I would not fear being with a concubine every night and we would have great mutual bliss. My body would not become debilitated, nor would I die young!
chapterbody 20: As I (Grandmaster Lu) see it:
chapterbody 21: Non-leakage Practice is a vital inner practice of Vajrayana (second-level empowerment), as well as an important dharma. Without Non-leakage Practice, how could one engage in Highest Yoga Tantra (third-level empowerment)? In non-leakage practice, one expels only qi not essence. When qi is abundant, channels open, inner fire rises, and the light drops (essence) sustain.
chapterbody 23: Therefore, only those who have accomplished Non-leakage Practice are qualified to engage in consort practice. The two major conditions needed to engage in consort practice are:
chapterbody 24: Therefore a true practitioner of yoga (a yogi) must accomplish Non-leakage Practice.
chapterbody 45: Non-leakage.

chaptername: 019 True Non-leakage Practice, Part 2
chapterbody: omeone asked, 'Which comes first? Cultivation of qi or cultivation of non-leakage?' I answered, 'Non-leakage practice requires the use of qi. Therefore, one should cultivate qi and non-leakage at the same time.'
chapterbody 5: The key cultivation formula of non-leakage is as follows:
chapterbody 6: 1. Fiercely sucking the middle finger.
chapterbody 7: When initial bliss (feeling sensitive) is sensed, one must be alert and immediately fiercely suck the middle finger. This transfers one's attention to the middle finger and diminishes the sensitivity of the initial bliss. This is one method of retaining essence (semen).
chapterbody 9: 2. Fiercely slapping the body.
chapterbody 10: When initial bliss is sensed, one must be alert, and quickly slap one's body until it hurts. This redirects one's attention to the painful area and diminishes the sensitivity of the initial bliss. This is another method of retaining essence.
chapterbody 12: 3. Forming Vajra Fist Mudra
chapterbody 13: When initial bliss is sensed, one must be alert. Both hands rapidly form a fist respectively with the thumbs inside the fists. Squeeze the thumbs until they hurt. This is to transfer the attention to the thumbs by firmly squeezing the Vajra Fist Mudra. As a result, the sensitivity of initial bliss diminishes. This is an additional method. (These three methods are methods of diverting attention)
chapterbody 15: 4. Sun and Moon facing heaven. (One of the Six Sakya Exercises of Non-leakage)
chapterbody 16: When initial bliss is sensed, one must be alert and have both the sun, the left eye, and the moon, the right eye, look upwards into the sky (ceiling). The first purpose is to divert attention. The second purpose is to ascend the qi. The sensitivity of initial bliss will then diminish.
chapterbody 18: 5. Pressing the tip of the tongue against the upper palate. (The second position of the Six Sakya Exercises of Non-leakage)
chapterbody 19: Press the tip of the tongue against the upper palate. This is the key cultivation formula of meditation (commonly called an overbridge). This forms a connection for the qi to shoot upward.
chapterbody 21: 6. Depleting Upper-body Qi [Udana]. (The third position of the Six Sakya Exercises of Non-leakage)
chapterbody 22: There are five kinds of qi in the human body:
chapterbody 23: Upper-body Qi—Controlling upper body.
chapterbody 24: Lower-body Qi [Apana]—Controlling lower body.
chapterbody 25: Circulating Qi [Viyana]—Circulating throughout the entire body.
chapterbody 26: Balancing Qi [Samana]—Inner fire that follows the central channel.
chapterbody 27: Heart Qi [Prana]—immovable at the heart.
chapterbody 28: As one exhales the upper-body qi, the lower-body qi will ascend and hold up the light drops (semen).
chapterbody 30: 7. Pressing the throat. (The fourth position of the Six Sakya Exercises of Non-leakage)
chapterbody 31: I often compare the principle of this method to the principle of a straw. After water is drawn into a straw, one can press the top of the straw with a finger to stop the water from flowing out. This is the same as one exhaling upper-body qi followed by pressing the chin down toward the throat to stop the light drops from flowing out.
chapterbody 33: 8. Tucking the belly toward the back (The fifth position of the Six Sakya Exercises of Non-leakage)
chapterbody 34: It is like tucking in one's stomach in the military. Form the abdomen into the shape of a bow. Lift and hold the light drops.
chapterbody 36: 9. Lift the anus upwards (The sixth position of the Six Sakya Exercises of Non-leakage)
chapterbody 37: Lifting the anus is also the practice of lifting. This causes the Lower-body Qi to ascend, prevents the light drops from flowing out, and locks the light drops.
chapterbody 42: One must practice these methods often. When necessary, comprehend the practice thoroughly and accomplish it at one stretch. The key point is at 'lifting', not at 'bearing'. One 'lifting' is a yogi, whereas one 'bearing' is an ordinary being.
chapterbody 43: This method involves practicing qi. If practicing consistently, one will be able to master it. One will then naturally accomplish the entire exercise within a thought. (This is the most essential key cultivation formula.)

chaptername: 020 True Non-leakage Practice, Part 3
chapterbody: disciple asked, 'Is Non-leakage Practice Buddhadharma?' I replied, 'What is not Buddhadharma?' (I turned the question back on the disciple)
chapterbody 2: I continued, 'Sex, anger, and ignorance are all Buddhadharma.' (Greed, anger, and ignorance)
chapterbody 4: The disciple asked, 'What does one cultivate through sexual activity?'
chapterbody 5: 'Buddha.'
chapterbody 7: 'What does one cultivate through anger?'
chapterbody 8: 'Vajra.'
chapterbody 10: 'What does one cultivate through ignorance?'
chapterbody 11: 'Bodhisattva.'
chapterbody 13: 'Has Grandmaster Lu discussed Non-leakage Practice in any book?'
chapterbody 14: 'I discussed this practice in Vajrayana Upadesa: Pith Instructions to Yogas of Spiritual Cultivation [Book 122] and Emptiness Within Great Bliss-An Explication of the Hevajra Tantra [Book 201].'
chapterbody 16: 'What is the teaching of Non-leakage Practice?'
chapterbody 17: 'Control of sexual thoughts, transformation into emptiness.'
chapterbody 19: 'Is Non-leakage Practice the preliminary practice of Consort Practice?'
chapterbody 20: 'Correct.'
chapterbody 22: 'Nowadays, the Consort Practice of Vajrayana is extensively criticized. What is Grandmaster's opinion of this?'
chapterbody 23: 'This is because worldly people have no understanding of the transcendent meaning of this practice. This practice is actually extremely pure. In the consort practice, intercourse between a worldly man and woman is transformed into a sacred practice.'
chapterbody 25: 'Is this possible?'
chapterbody 26: 'Yes, absolutely. The Consort Practice contains visualization, mudra skills, mantra, cultivation of qi, inner fire practice, chakra practice, light drops practice, clear light practice, entering samadhi practice'
chapterbody 28: 'Is it difficult to imagine?'
chapterbody 29: 'An ordinary person has no way of knowing that great bliss, clear light, emptiness, and even attainment of Buddhahood in this very body can all be cultivated through the Consort Practice.'
chapterbody 31: 'What is the key cultivation formula of this practice?'
chapterbody 32: 'Relax, calmly abide, meditate properly, empty everything, think of nothing, attain great bliss, transform into light, annihilate all phenomena, and realize emptiness.'
chapterbody 34: 'Are there evil gurus who use the Consort Practice to cheat women?'
chapterbody 35: 'This is difficult to avoid. However, this kind of person is easy to recognize.'
chapterbody 38: 'Has the person received the third-level empowerment of Highest Yoga Tantra? (Recognized empowerment by the Root Guru) Has the person attained non-leakage? Does the person have the right thoughts?'

chaptername: 021 The King of Great Bliss
chapterbody: ne day, I calmed my mind and entered samadhi. Vajrasattva told me, 'You are the king of great bliss!'
chapterbody 3: I asked, 'Why?'
chapterbody 4: Vajrasattva replied, 'You are the king of great bliss because you offer great bliss to self-nature, buddhas, bodhisattvas, vajra deities, Dharma protectors, dakinis, and heavens.'
chapterbody 6: I asked, 'According to what I know, isn't circumambulating a stupa three times or performing any other type of offerings making an offering?'
chapterbody 7: 'A stupa symbolizes buddhas, so one must, of course, make offerings to it. When performing all other offering practices, such as circumambulating a mountain, a lake, or a temple, one must, of course, make an offering. However, these offerings are not formless.'
chapterbody 9: 'Then, how should we make offerings?'
chapterbody 10: 'You and everyone should make offerings to those who abide by the precepts, meditate properly, have buddha wisdom, are liberated, and attain the true wisdom of liberation.'
chapterbody 11: 'Indeed, we should.'
chapterbody 13: 'I have a question for you, Grandmaster Lu. What is the form of so-called precepts, meditation, wisdom, liberation, and the wisdom of liberation?'
chapterbody 14: 'Formless.'
chapterbody 16: 'Since they are formless, how do you make offerings to them?'
chapterbody 17: 'Offer what is formless to that which is formless.'
chapterbody 19: 'Does your great bliss have form?'
chapterbody 20: 'No, it is formless.' (One cannot take hold of great bliss)
chapterbody 22: 'Because it is formless, it is the supreme offering. Do you understand?'
chapterbody 23: I somewhat understood.
chapterbody 25: Vajrasattva told me:
chapterbody 26: 'The Buddha said that all sentient beings are the objects of offering. One must make offerings to self-nature. The self-nature is buddha. This is equivalent to offering to buddha. Please take note that learning all that tathagatas have learned is the supreme offering. Making Dharma offerings is the foremost.
chapterbody 27: 'Cultivating the self-nature to attain Buddhahood is an offering. Therefore, one should be without attachment, no self-grasping, no attachment to Dharma, and no attachment to form.'
chapterbody 29: I said, 'Beginners might not understand this.'
chapterbody 30: 'Indeed, beginners don't comprehend non-arising, non-doing, and non-duality.'
chapterbody 32: 'So, how to enlighten sentient beings?'
chapterbody 33: Vajrasattva commented, 'This is why I call you the king of great bliss. You make an offering of great bliss to self-nature and all the tathagatas. This is the true non-leakage offering.'

chaptername: 022 Details of Nine-Cycle Breathing
chapterbody: ine-Cycle Breathing can also be called Nine-Round Breathing. The sadhana is as follows:
chapterbody 3: 1. Begin by performing the seven preliminary steps.
chapterbody 4: 2.Visualize that the entire body is as transparent and pure as glass.
chapterbody 5: There are three vertical channels parallel to each other within one's body. The three channels originate in the navel chakra, four fingers below the navel. The central channel is in the shape of a trumpet. The mouth of the trumpet is located at the crown of the head with the lower part of the trumpet tapering down from the top. The left, right, and central channels run parallel to each other. The left channel connects to the left nostril, and the right channel connects with the right nostril. (This concludes visualization of the three channels.)
chapterbody 6: 3. Press the left nostril with the left ring finger. Inhale through the right nostril.
chapterbody 7: 4. Visualize the light of buddhas and bodhisattvas illumina'ting the entire space and one is inhaling this light.
chapterbody 8: 5. Inhale a complete breath through the right nostril. Press the right ring finger against the right nostril.
chapterbody 9: 6. Visualize the light circulating downwards through the ri'ght channel to the navel chakra. The light then turns upward through the left channel to the left nostril.
chapterbody 10: 7. Exhale through the left nostril.
chapterbody 11: (At this time, practitioner visualizes all karmic hindrances, sickness, ignorance, and afflictions transforming into black smoke which is exhaled through the left nostril.) (Repeat the first cycle, steps 3 - 7, three times.)
chapterbody 12: 8. Next, press the right ring finger against the right nostril. Inhale the bright light through the left nostril. The light [descends the left channel] then enters the right channel. Exhale black smoke out the right nostril. The steps are the same as the first three cycles, except the left and right nostrils are exchanged. (Repeat the second cycle, step 8, three times.)
chapterbody 13: 9. Last cycle: Inhale the bright light qi through both the left and right channels. The light qi merges into one at the navel chakra, enters the central channel, and ascends to the crown where there is no opening for the qi. The qi circulates downward through the central channel to the navel chakra, four fingers below the navel, where it enters the left and right channels and is exhaled as black smoke from the left and right nostrils. (Perform the last cycle, step 9, three times) These nine cycles are called Nine-Cycle Breathing or Nine-Round Breathing.
chapterbody 14: 10. Mantra.
chapterbody 15: Om, bie-zha, sa-do, ah, hum-pei.
chapterbody 16: (Recite 108 or more times)
chapterbody 17: 11. Enter Samadhi.
chapterbody 18: 12. Emerge from Samadhi.
chapterbody 19: 13. Dedication
chapterbody 20: May this merit quickly transform into the accomplishment of Buddhahood.
chapterbody 21: May my karmic hindrances and that of all sentient beings be purified.
chapterbody 22: May the three karmas transform into the three secrets.
chapterbody 23: May sentient beings of the six realms attain liberation.
chapterbody 24: 14. Great Homage.
chapterbody 25: 15. Exit the shrine.
chapterbody 27: I (Grandmaster Lu) say:
chapterbody 28: This Nine-Cycle Breathing is peaceful and won't cause any harm. Visualize light being like space, because the tathagatas are omnipresent. Upon inhalation, the practitioner accomplishes the six great non-hindrances, merging of emptiness and existence, and unification of self and buddha. Karmic transgressions are purified, and the practitioner attains a state of concentration.
chapterbody 29: In this practice, the best method is to practice with concentration. To easily reach concentration, one must coordinate the fingers with one's breathing. Inhale white and exhale black. Over time, one will feel light and refreshed. After sufficient practice, one will be able to control one's breathing through the left and right nostrils without the use of one's fingers. This will make the practice easier.

chaptername: 023 Manjusri Bodhisattva and Sariputra
chapterbody: ccording to the Sutra of the Buddha's Discourse on Manjusri's Worthy Manifestations, Manjusri and Sariputra toured southern buddha lands. Together, after flying over millions of billions of realms, they came to the pure land of The Solemn Land of Debao Tathagata.
chapterbody 3: Manjusri asked, 'Venerable Sariputra, how did you perceive the realms we flew over?'
chapterbody 4: Sariputra answered, 'Some were totally engulfed in fire, some were completely underwater, some were empty illusions, some were rich and prosperous, some were impoverished, some were just developing, some were already destroyed, some were evil, some were virtuous''
chapterbody 6: Manjusri asked, 'Venerable Sariputra, what are your thoughts and impressions?'
chapterbody 7: Sariputra replied, 'Engulfed in flames is engulfed in flames, underwater is underwater... Should I have some other impression?'
chapterbody 9: Manjusri said, 'All of the realms were the lands of void. Fire, water, prosperity, poverty, developing, disintegrating, evil, and virtue are all unreal. These are temporary manifestations caused by external conditions.
chapterbody 10: Sariputra! By analogy, the mind of every practitioner is temporarily contaminated by ignorance and afflictions, but self-nature (Buddha'nature) is like emptiness, eternal and free of contamination.
chapterbody 11: Sariputra! Regardless of how realms are changing, emptiness is unaffected. Likewise, Buddha-nature is never contaminated. For this reason, I (Manjusri) am never contaminated. I am able to abide within the absolute purity of self-nature.'
chapterbody 13: Upon hearing this, Sariputra attained a great awakening.
chapterbody 17: Grandmaster Lu says:
chapterbody 18: These words of Manjusri Bodhisattva are the supreme key cultivation secret. Contamination is just an outer appearance. Buddha-nature is eternally free of contamination.
chapterbody 19: Take watching a movie as an example. While watching a movie, I see scenes of tortuous grievances (wealth, desire, fame, food, and sleep), greed, anger, and ignorance. However, when the movie is over, I am still me. Those grievances have nothing to do with me. Greed, anger, and ignorance also become irrelevant. I use 'non-interference' to explain everything.
chapterbody 21: Today, learning Buddhism, studying Vajrayana, and cultivating Nine-Cycle Breathing have four objectives:
chapterbody 23: As for my explanation of this key cultivation formula, this 'direct pointing', everyone should have some realization.
chapterbody 45: Concentration.

chaptername: 024 Vajra Chanting
chapterbody: randmaster Lu says: Vajra Chanting can be subdivided into the following methods: Vajra Chanting by qi through the nose. Vajra Chanting by qi through the lower gate. Vajra Chanting by merging upper and lower qi. Vajra Chanting by qi through the nose and lower gate. Vajra Chanting by circulating qi throughout the body. Vajra Chanting by qi through a sense faculty.
chapterbody 5: The key cultivation formula is: qi entering, holding, and exiting.
chapterbody 9: Merits of Vajra Chanting are:
chapterbody 10: (For example, whereas an ordinary person would need 21 days to travel through the mountains, Venerable Milarepa, because of practicing Vajra Chanting, only needed three days.)
chapterbody 14: Mantra syllables are:
chapterbody 15: Om 
chapterbody 16: Ah 
chapterbody 17: Hum 
chapterbody 21: How to practice (example):
chapterbody 22: To perform Vajra Chanting, when inhaling through the nose, one first visualizes oneself as Vajrasattva with a consort, with three channels and five chakras. There is a sun disc within the eight-petaled lotus in the heart chakra. In the center of the sun disc is a moon disc. Above the moon disc is a white light drop above a red light drop and the light drops are about the size of a pea. Above the red and white light drops, there is a bright blue 'hum' syllable. One visualizes the above in three dimensions.
chapterbody 23: When the qi enters, it enters as an Om syllable. When the qi is held, it's held as an Ah syllable. When the qi exits, it exits as a Hum syllable.
chapterbody 27: Pay close attention to this key cultivation formula:
chapterbody 28: When the qi enters, it ascends to the crown chakra, then flows to the throat chakra, the heart chakra, the navel chakra, and then to the sacral chakra. The qi then returns to abide in the heart chakra. One should hold the qi in the heart chakra as long as possible.
chapterbody 29: Inhale a long breath. Exhale a short breath. The qi enters detailed, slow, and long. The qi exits detailed, slow, and short. While holding the qi, visualize the mantra syllable emitting white qi surrounded by five-colored light like an enshrouding mist.
chapterbody 30: (The 16th Karmapa said that maintaining light in the heart is most important. This is a bright light within the heart.)
chapterbody 34: By holding the qi in Vajra Chanting, qi enters the central channel and ignites the inner fire. This leads to the generation of great bliss, which then produces bright light. One visualizes emptiness to reach the unified cultivation of bliss and emptiness.
chapterbody 35: These are my essential key cultivation formulas derived from my own practice of Vajra Chanting. They are very, very important.
chapterbody 45: Opening up channels.
chapterbody 49: Body as light as a sparrow.
chapterbody 54: Opening channel knots in the heart chakra.

chaptername: 025 Treasure Vase Breathing
chapterbody: uru Padmasambhava said, 'All merits are derived from Treasure Vase Breathing.'
chapterbody 2: Grandmaster Lu says, 'Just as Guru Padmasambhava stated, all merits are derived from 'holding qi' in the Treasure Vase Breathing Practice.'
chapterbody 6: What is Treasure Vase Breathing?
chapterbody 7: It is interlocking one's qi by pressing down the Upper-body qi while lifting the Lower-body qi. This will cause the qi to be held within, like the shape of a treasure vase. Therefore, this is called Treasure Vase Breathing.
chapterbody 11: What is the merit of cultivating Treasure Vase Breathing?
chapterbody 15: Key cultivation formula of Treasure Vase Breathing:
chapterbody 16: When one can no longer hold the qi, visualize qi as blue in color, shooting up to the crown (opening the crown). Any remaining qi exits through the nostrils.
chapterbody 19: Key points when cultivating Treasure Vase Breathing:
chapterbody 20: Most females cultivate Treasure Vase Breathing in the heart chakra.
chapterbody 21: Most males cultivate Treasure Vase Breathing beneath the navel.
chapterbody 22: This is because a female who holds qi beneath the navel when cultivating Treasure Vase Breathing may experience disruption of the menstrual cycle or Menometrorrhagia.
chapterbody 23: When cultivating Treasure Vase Breathing, I emphasize gentle breathing; that is, gently performing inhaling, filling, and expelling.
chapterbody 24: I do not cultivate 'fierce' qi (rhythmic breathing), because this does more harm than good.
chapterbody 25: (For example, Master Lianxuan taught fierce qi (rhythmic breathing), and as a result, suffered a premature death.)
chapterbody 26: Caution: Shooting qi to the crown can only be performed in the first cycle. To avoid adverse consequences or premature death, do not repeat this shooting in the following cycles.
chapterbody 29: Key cultivation formula of inhalation:
chapterbody 30: In space, there is rainbow light. One completely inhales this light long, slow, and detailed down to four fingers below the navel. One tightens one's anus and lifts the Lower-body qi upwards. The upper and lower qi merge four fingers below the navel.
chapterbody 31: Press the upper qi downwards.
chapterbody 32: Lift the lower qi.
chapterbody 33: Interlock them to form a treasure vase.
chapterbody 38: Hold qi in the treasure vase.
chapterbody 39: Swallow saliva, which is also pressing down.
chapterbody 40: Hold the qi for 36 'finger snaps.' Gradually increase to 72 counts, then 108 counts.
chapterbody 41: (When doing this, one should go at one's own pace without forcing it. Self-observe and adjust one's pace to what works best.)
chapterbody 42: When holding qi, qi in the left and right channels will directly enter the central channel. Let things happen naturally. While visualizing, apply the mind to direct the qi into the central channel.
chapterbody 45: Qi enters the central channel.
chapterbody 53: Totally unobstructed flow of qi through the central channel.
chapterbody 54: While holding one's qi, breathing through the nose is halted, and thoughts do not arise.

chaptername: 026 Spiritual Sensations from Treasure Vase Breathing
chapterbody: n this chapter, I will describe the spiritual sensations of Treasure Vase Breathing. This concerns the experiences I had after attaining spiritual union with the principal deity. (Spiritual sensations personally experienced by Grandmaster Lu)
chapterbody 5: Spiritual sensations while inhaling:
chapterbody 6: I discovered infinite numbers of great bodhisattvas, great vajra deities, and many Dharma protectors in the void. I also came across Mahabrahma, Indra, the yaksa king, Citrasena, and many others. Surprisingly, they followed my inhalation and entered my body like a string of pearls.
chapterbody 7: In addition, surrounding me on all sides were celestial gods of body, foot, and earth. Included were city gods, district gods, mountain gods, water gods, lake gods, tree gods, earth gods, and many more.
chapterbody 9: Spiritual sensations of filling:
chapterbody 10: I seemed to live in a palace made of the seven jewels, sitting in a full-lotus position upon a jeweled throne. My body was fully adorned with the marks and signs of a buddha as well as adornments of body and clothing.
chapterbody 11: At this time, there was an utter purity beyond anything imaginable on earth or in heaven. An orb of pure radiant light emerged from my crown. There was light behind me. My entire body was surrounded by perfect light. Above the orb of light was a jeweled canopy. Beneath my feet were lotuses of many colors. A rain of flowers fell from the sky. The heavens resounded with exquisite music. Clouds of offerings descended from the sky. Rainbows arched in the sky. The heavens had the taste of Dharma. I accomplished infinite merit.
chapterbody 13: Spiritual sensations of expelling:
chapterbody 14: This was a unique inner spiritual sensation. My mind was pure, clear, and apart from all faults and afflictions. Obstacles no longer existed. All the meritorious roots of a bodhisattva were accomplished. My faith and compassion increased exponentially. I entered deep and profound Prajnaparamita. Every pore of my body emitted rays of light. In every ray of light was a buddha.
chapterbody 16: Spiritual sensations of shooting:
chapterbody 17: I was replete with every majesty, wonderful sound, marvelous form, and spiritual transformation. I continuously discoursed Buddhadharma without ever losing my aspiration. The content of my discourses included virtuous roots, right Dharma, cultivation of the bodhisattva paramitas, purification, subjugation of the four maras, equality, oneness, and no mind.
chapterbody 21: Concerning these spiritual sensations of mine, Vajrasattva exclaimed, 'This has never happened before!'

chaptername: 027 The Key Cultivation Formula of Inner Fire
chapterbody: y cultivating Treasure Vase Breathing, the inner fire is ignited in the Dharma Producing Palace, four fingers below the navel. I describe the key cultivation formula as follows:
chapterbody 3: 1. Sit in the Six-firepit Mudra Sitting Position.
chapterbody 4: 2. Inhaling, filling, expelling, and shooting, the key cultivation formula of Treasure Vase Breathing, is actually the key cultivation formula of inner fire as well.
chapterbody 5: 3. Strengthening one's qi during Treasure Vase Breathing is the practice of inner fire.
chapterbody 6: 4. In principle, tinder for the inner fire is originally located in the area four fingers below the navel.
chapterbody 7: 5. Apply the 'blast furnace' method to ignite the tinder into light, brilliance, and heat that is red and brilliant, like hot steel.
chapterbody 8: 6. The method is as follows:
chapterbody 9: Rubbing—
chapterbody 10: Twisting—Turn your body to twist the Dharma Producing Palace.
chapterbody 11: Pressing—Use your hands to press the Dharma Producing Palace.
chapterbody 12: Tightening—Corset the waist with a Zen belt.
chapterbody 13: 7. One utilizes every available method to cause the inner fire to ignite.
chapterbody 14: 8. At first, the fire is thin and sharp (like a copper needle).
chapterbody 15: 9. After the inner fire is ignited, it resembles a pine cone.
chapterbody 16: 10. First, keep it burning throughout the navel chakra for 10 breaths. Next, lower the fire to the sacral chakra and let it burn for 10 breaths. Third, burn the heart chakra. (10 breaths) Fourth, burn the throat chakra. (10 breaths) Fifth, burn the brow chakra. (10 breaths) Sixth, burn throughout one's entire body. Seventh, burn the area of one's feet. Eighth, the fire spreads to all the pores of one's body.
chapterbody 17: 11. Everywhere the inner fire reaches, filth and contamination are entirely burned away and the pure self-nature is restored.
chapterbody 18: 12. Inner fire is like an electric light spreading throughout one's entire body. One feels the sensation of firelight emitted from all the pores of one's entire body.
chapterbody 19: 13. Inner fire is emitted to the apex of heaven, the great earth, all sentient beings, and the three evil realms, filling all with blazing brilliance.
chapterbody 20: 14. I, Grandmaster Lu, say:
chapterbody 21: One's qi connects with the qi of the void. My channels are the entirety of space. The entirety of space is inner fire. The entire space is light drops. (Regarding Venerable Milarepa's attainment of inner fire, I asked Padmasambhava a question: By what power did Milarepa fly through space? Padmasambhava answered that, as Milarepa's inner fire burned away all impurities and obstacles, his body became like space and his guru, personal deity, Dharma protector, and their retinues, all entered his body. It was through the power of Milarepa's guru, personal deity, Dharma protector, dakinis, and their retinues that he was able to fly through space.)
chapterbody 22: 15. It is written in the Buddhist sutras that when an arhat reaches attainment, his upper body emits fire and his lower body emits water. Or, his upper body emits water and his lower body emits fire. What causes this to happen? I replied that fire means inner fire. Water refers to light drops. In Vajrayana, practitioners validate 'true emptiness is marvelous existence' by utilizing the expedient means of qi, channels, and light drops.
chapterbody 23: 16. Expedient means is the cause, buddha wisdom is the result. Cultivate inner fire through concentration. Practice diligently with great faith. Follow the key cultivation formulas transmitted by the guru. Visualize and attain the treasure of meditation and brilliant light.
chapterbody 24: 17. In cultivating inner fire, there are many key cultivation formulas. As a beginner, a disciple won't know these formulas. One should be close with Grandmaster Lu to receive these key cultivation formulas personally. Some must be supplemented by Vajra Fist.
chapterbody 33: Rub the area four fingers below the navel.

chaptername: 028 The Key Cultivation Formula of Light Drops
chapterbody: . What are light drops? Light drops can be explained in many ways, most generally as body fluids, water, hormones, essence, and bodhicitta moon fluid.

chapterbody 3: 3. There are four forms of light drops:
chapterbody 4: White as crystal clear.
chapterbody 5: Bright as a lamp.
chapterbody 6: Drops about the size of a ping pong ball.
chapterbody 7: Drops cascading down like mercury.

chapterbody 10: 6. This happiness is the sensation of bliss which we call great bliss. These blisses, produced by qi flowing through one's channels, inner fire flowing through one's channels, and descending light drops, are namely the Four Blisses.
chapterbody 11: 7. This great bliss is caused by friction. It is similar to the tingling ecstasy of sexual pleasure.
chapterbody 12: 8. Use qi and inner fire to lift the light drops upwards through the navel chakra, heart chakra, throat chakra, to the brow chakra.
chapterbody 13: This can be referred to as the Four Blisses. It can also be called the four levels of emptiness, emptiness, great emptiness, supreme emptiness, most supreme emptiness.
chapterbody 14: (resulting from non-dual emptiness and bliss)
chapterbody 15: 9. I Grandmaster Lu, say:
chapterbody 16: According to what I know: Supernatural power will definitely arise upon opening the brow chakra, throat chakra, heart chakra, navel chakra, and sacral chakra. Supernatural power of divine feet. Supernatural power of divine eye. Supernatural power of divine hearing. Supernatural power of knowing the thoughts of others. Supernatural power of knowing one's and others' past lives. (The supreme supernatural power is total mastery of non-leakage). One is replete with the Five Eyes and Six Supernatural Powers.
chapterbody 17: 10. Inner fire, light drops, and qi merge as one and spread throughout the channels of one's entire body, even to the pores. Instantaneously, universal brilliant light is produced.
chapterbody 18: 11. A halo of light appears at the crown. One's entire body is surrounded by light from the back. There is lotus light beneath one's feet.
chapterbody 19: 12. Here I will further describe the Six-firepit Mudra:
chapterbody 20: Outer Six-firepit Mudra—cross wrists, cross feet, and press calves with the crossed hands.
chapterbody 21: Inner Six-firepit Mudra—Dharma Producing Palace with its six triangles.
chapterbody 22: Qi Six-firepit Mudra. (easy to ignite inner fire)
chapterbody 23: 13. As for inner fire and light drops, the key cultivation formula is that qi, visualization, thoughts, and Vajra Fist must all coordinate and work together. When they do, one will have attainment in cultivation. (Vajra Fist is supplementary)

chaptername: 029 A Truly Amazing Spiritual Transformation
chapterbody: ccording to my knowledge, when the three channels and seven chakras, and all the channels in the body open completely, a great spiritual transformation that is difficult to believe will result. The three channels are the left channel, right channel, and central channel. The seven chakras are the crown chakra, brow chakra, throat chakra, heart chakra, navel chakra, sacral chakra, and root chakra. There are 72,000 channels in the body.
chapterbody 3: The great spiritual transformation can be described as follows:
chapterbody 4: Ability to see stars in the universe as numerous as the sands of the Ganges. One can see and have knowledge of all the brightly illuminating stars in the mundane realms and in the amazing cosmic realms as well.
chapterbody 5: One is able to visit Maha Twin Lotus Ponds, the Huayan Pure Land, the Western Pure Land of Utmost Bliss, the Pure Land of Lapis Lazuli, the Abhirati Pure Land, the Pure Land of Perfect Penetration, the Cuiwei Pure Land and countless other pure lands without the slightest hindrance.
chapterbody 6: One can listen to Dharma discourses from one's personal deity as well as from all the tathagatas of the ten directions and three times.
chapterbody 7: One will be able to see the seven past buddhas, the 1,000 buddhas of the bhadra kalpa, and all the infinite buddhas. One will also see Shakyamuni Buddha, Dipamkara Buddha, and Maitreya Buddha manifesting on a lion Dharma throne.
chapterbody 8: One will be able to treat everything in the material world as nothing, and pass through it without damaging oneself or the objects.
chapterbody 9: One can enter into mountains, oceans, and space.
chapterbody 10: One can pour all the waters of the four great oceans into a single pore of one's body.
chapterbody 11: One can transform a huge mountain into a mustard seed, and a mustard seed into a huge mountain.
chapterbody 12: One can stretch out the palm, and place all the sentient beings of the six realms on the palm without harming the life of any sentient being. (Sentient beings are unaware.)
chapterbody 13: One will be able to move the sun, the moon, and the stars.
chapterbody 14: One's own light can overshadow the light of the sun, the moon, and the stars.
chapterbody 15: One can transform the Mara, Papiyan, into the Buddha.
chapterbody 16: One can transform the Buddha into the Mara.
chapterbody 17: One can know the absolute truth.
chapterbody 18: One can place the trichiliocosm in a grain of rice.
chapterbody 19: One can expand a grain of rice into the vastness of space.
chapterbody 20: These illusory transformations are called 'The samadhi of illusory transformation of myriad dharmas'.
chapterbody 21: To my knowledge, I never move. I am still at South Mountain Retreat, but also at Maha Twin Lotus Ponds where all forms, images, people, and everything else vividly appear.
chapterbody 23: Divine eye, divine ear, knowing past lives, knowing others' thoughts, and divine feet are all minor supernatural powers.
chapterbody 24: What's important is that I have learned the absolute truth. All dharmas are the self-nature of the Dharma realm. No contamination no purity, no binding no liberation, no origination no extinction, no coming no going, no oneness no duality, no gain no loss, nothing to gain, nothing matters, and non-abiding.
chapterbody 25: (No self, no people, no sentient beings, no longevity)

chaptername: 030 Illusory Samadhi
chapterbody: cultivate illusory samadhi with Mirror Gazing Yoga. Practitioner sits in front of a large mirror on a wall. An image of the personal deity is hung above the mirror. At this time, the practitioner is outside the mirror. An image of the practitioner is inside the mirror. The personal deity is above the mirror.
chapterbody 3: The cultivation method is as follows:
chapterbody 4: One visualizes oneself transforming into the personal deity. In great detail, one visualizes that, one by one, the eyes, ears, nose, tongue, body, mind, and self transform into the personal deity. During this time, the personal deity is above the mirror, within the mirror, and outside the mirror. The three merge into one and enter samadhi, meaning quiet contemplation and meditation. The three transform into one entity.
chapterbody 5: One then thinks that I am not real, the 'I' in the mirror is not real, and the personal deity above the mirror is not real. From this, one will validate that the six roots, the six consciousnesses, and the six dusts are not real. They are nothing but illusory phenomena of cause and effect.
chapterbody 6: By practicing this method, one validates that illusions, dreams, echoes in the valley, dew, water bubbles, mirages, and rainbows are all unreal. (none have self-nature) Body is illusory. Speech is illusory. Mind is illusory. As a result, one detaches from the 'eight worldly concerns'. (For example, Venerable Milarepa, entered illusory samadhi, detached from the eight worldly concerns, and accomplished non-leakage. He wrote songs about his detachment from the eight worldly concerns.)
chapterbody 8: The eight worldly concerns are gain (wealth, form, and fame), loss (also wealth, form, and fame), fame, slander, praise, mockery, suffering and happiness. Practitioners who depart from the eight worldly concerns have established firm and unshakeable faith. Not just that, they also utterly sever attachment to self and Dharma.
chapterbody 10: Whether a senior practitioner or not, one must visualize in the following way. I am like an illusory person. I am like the moon's reflection in water. I am like a form, a shadow, a dream, clouds, a rainbow, electricity, light, a reflection in a mirror, and so forth.
chapterbody 11: By accomplishing illusory samadhi, one becomes indestructible, just like a vajra.
chapterbody 12: By severing ignorance and deluded attachments, one attains pure self-nature and non-duality, and accomplishes the reward body.

chaptername: 031 Key Cultivation Formula of Dream Yoga
chapterbody: y favorite poem is 'Dreams' by Wang Anshi:
chapterbody 3: In this dreamlike world, there is nothing to desire
chapterbody 4: A heart without desires is boundless, empty, and serene
chapterbody 5: Roaming through this dreamscape
chapterbody 6: Accomplishing dreamlike merit as infinite as sands in the river
chapterbody 10: In my opinion, this mundane world is just a 100-year dream, a journey from birth to death through schooling, career, marriage, children, aging, and illness. All these phenomena, including samsara and nirvana, are without true meaning. If observing on an infinitesimal scale, we discover these goals cannot be attained. Everything is as illusory as a dream and one should not be attached.
chapterbody 11: (By cultivating dreams, one can attain emptiness)
chapterbody 15: To cultivate dream yoga, one must first purify one's karmic hindrances. If one does not purify one's karmic hindrances, obstacles will arise.
chapterbody 16: Practitioners of dream yoga must repent violations of the precepts or the samaya vow. If one does not purify transgressions through repentance, obstacles will arise.
chapterbody 17: Practitioners of dream yoga must generate enormous and supremely reverent faith toward the root guru and Vajrayana. Without reverent faith, one will accomplish nothing.
chapterbody 18: Practitioners of dream yoga must perform boundary protection if the residence is dirty, equipment is filthy, food or clothing is unclean, partner is toxic, bodhicitta is leaking, environment is impure, or people are coming and going.
chapterbody 22: Practitioners of dream yoga must cultivate inner fire and use inner fire to burn away their afflictions and thoughts.
chapterbody 23: (This is the key cultivation formula)
chapterbody 27: My method of cultivation is as follows:
chapterbody 28: Time: cultivate prior to sleeping or when one wakes up in the middle of the night.
chapterbody 29: 1. Visualize the root guru at one's throat.
chapterbody 30: 2. Pray for the blessings of the root guru so that one may cultivate and generate good karma in one's dreams.
chapterbody 31: 3. Visualize a four-petaled lotus in the throat chakra. In the center of the lotus, there is a red 'om' syllable.
chapterbody 32: 4. Or, one may additionally visualize a syllable on each petal of the four-petaled lotus:
chapterbody 33: Center—'om', in white color.
chapterbody 34: Front petal—'ah', in blue color.
chapterbody 35: Right petal—'lu', in yellow color.
chapterbody 38: 5. Practice 'entering, holding, and exiting' of Vajra Chanting, and recite 'om' only.
chapterbody 42: One must first comprehend that everything is just a dream, both day and night. One's mind is extremely clear and aware it is dreaming. One is able to transform an evil dream into a virtuous dream. Or, one is able to cultivate Buddhadharma in one's dream. Eventually, one is able to control dreams and practice Buddhadharma in dreams.

chaptername: 032 Supremely Pure Light Shining Up to the Sky
chapterbody: ne day, Living Buddha Lian-sheng Sheng-Yen Lu was sitting peacefully on his Dharma seat at South Mountain Retreat. While in true samadhi, a meditative state free of thoughts, my body suddenly disappeared.
chapterbody 2: Then, a supremely pure light shone up to the sky. This supremely pure clear light caused six kinds of earthquakes in all the infinite purelands of the ten directions. This supremely pure clear light manifested an enormous, gentle, wonderful, and uncontaminated brilliant light which reached into all the ten dharma realms.
chapterbody 3: This light alarmed the celestial beings in Brahmas of End of Form, where at this time, the Buddha happened to be discoursing the Buddhadharma.
